item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Unfortunately, there is no indication the author has released the image under a license compliant with the wiki's (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the picture has been removed.

item.title: copyrights
item.content:encoded: it's good that you sourced the image correctly, but i looked through the artist's site and I don't see any indication that he made the photo available under a suitable license.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite the picture, I somehow find this skip very comforting. It seems like a nice entity.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
an attempt for further communication was attempted by overseeing researcher Dr. Montalvo.

i think the word 'attempt' should only be used once in this sentence, and it should instead look like:

further communication was attempted by overseeing researcher Dr. Montalvo.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP already has inline links to Herman Fuller, so I propose linking it to the hub

Editing this:

Searches of the surrounding area has recovered several non-anomalous artifacts, bearing labels of "Herman Fuller's Circus of the Disquieting".

To this:

Searches of the surrounding area has recovered several non-anomalous artifacts, bearing labels of "Herman Fuller's Circus of the Disquieting".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed most of this - especially the possible implications of the first Incident - but I'm not sure that having the SCP speak works quite so well. Makes it less enigmatic and haunting, in my opinion.

I didn't notice this fly past +200

thanks all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one is really saddening. The guy just wants to sing his heart out, but it can't because it has no audience to sing to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty interesting. I see the Euclid status isn't because it is overtly hostile or dangerous, but because if the fabric tears it can cause injury for the sound that is released. I feel it wants to be heard. Its purpose isn't just to sing, but to have an audience so as to not be forgotten.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this SCP. The music notes are a nice, interesting idea. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs a cross-link to http://www.scp-wiki.net/former-assets, please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, it's been a while since I last read this and I remembered what this thing did, but not much else about the article. Rereading it for March Madness… Now I'm sad. You made me sad. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Late response, but I intended it to be either way, it's or its.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is beautiful, +1.

I love how the picture makes you think it's screaming aggressively, but then you realize it's actually singing beautiful music. For some reason this reminded me of Klaus Nomi (voice of an angel, creepy look, tragic fate).

I'm curious what the article's name, "It's Bleeding Song", is supposed to mean, though - was this meant to be "Its Bleeding Song"? I feel like that would make more sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God damn that's a creepy picture.

I'll admit I came a little late to the circus. SO glad I found this! It's tragic. As with SCP-1834, he lives to perform (conditioned to?). Vocally speaking, I am reminded of the Diva from The Fifth Element. Would be interesting to see what it could do with more modern music.

Am so curious as to why the Circus lost this one. I would think it a centerpiece.

+1. As said previously, such a scary image, such a tragic story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one's really good. He just wants to sing and be heard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's actually kinda sad which is a good quality in SCPs. I'll up vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, good. I didn't want to change it in case it was intentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup, that was a typo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It should say Hellbrunner Allee, rather than Hellbrunner Aslee, shouldn't it? What's Hellbrunner Aslee?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops, didn't notice this post. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
in which SCP-1860-1 kept in Storage Sector-57.

This seems to be a sentence fragment? Might want to fix if so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much like others, as soon as I saw the picture I was expecting some creepy monster that kills people and all that, but I was pleasantly surprised as I continued to read. Nice job taking a creepy creature and creating something so original out of it. Poor lil dude, I actually feel kinda bad for him (it?). Definitely one of my favorites out of the Herman-Fuller list. Upvoted!

item.title: That Picture
item.content:encoded: That picture is fucking creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
More errors, yaaaay

Thanks for finding them, addressing them all now. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are still some typos and awkward phrases. I'll just list as I read and let you decide which, if any, actually need fixing.

..in a sound-proof containment chamber…

Drainage systems dispose of excess liquid, the clause explaining so is redundant.

…entity of 2.1 meters in height.

"of" makes the phrase awkward and the whole sentence could use a do-over. Maybe break it into 2 sentences and also tell us how much it weights?

Why "suggests masculinity"? I can see nipples, so anyone with a frontal view should be able to clearly tell if it is male or not. Unless it is lacking certain plumbing, in which case that should be noted.

The material is nothing like nylon. Or rather, it has some properties similar to most synthetic fabrics, not just nylon. It would read much smoother to just say it is an anomalously flexible and durable weave of paper and make no mention on nylon.

Those holes are the source of -1, there is no "appear to be" if the scientists can see it coming out.

In the same vein, he absolutely can TK the ink.

…at 0.6 meters above ground, in which they will orbit …

Suggested re-phrase "…ground and orbiting itself clockwise."

You mention it "usually" only makes musical notations, but there is never an example given of it sky writing anything else.

The table adds nothing but length. Scratch that, the songs help tell the story, but only if the reader knows the songs. It might be worthwhile to try and emphasis the importance of the song choices somehow, to encourage the readers to google so they'll get the point you're making. No idea how to do that though.

Nitpicking aside; this is odd, a bit melancholy, and creepy without being menacing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If like me, you don't know the song, take that advice and google it now. You'll be glad you did.
The lyrics in this context have a significant impact on the feel and tone of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yup, it sings by itself. I'll get to work on those things you pointed out.

Edit: I reworded those sentences, thanks for pointing them out. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ please fix these
- thanks

There does not to seem any underlying relationships regarding the choice of music, and includes songs from various countries and eras.

Upon showing no signs of starting

was produced during this performance, produced from SCP-1860's eyes.

I really like this. I was meh through the description, but the incidents swayed me. +1

Am I understanding correctly that there's no music playing? It sings, and there are notes hovering in the air, but there's no part of it that anomalously produces music.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, make me feel even worse. :( Great addendum, you monster.

item.title: Image Source
item.content:encoded:
Did some diggin, and I found the image was posted here. (Watch out, site is NSFW in many areas).

Apparently, it was photographed by Circle23. So yeah, go look at their stuff. If you don't like sleep

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
poor guy, somebody forgot about him…

Hell 8 liters just by crying, that's a lot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a big fan of the addition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added an incident, to show what kind of situation this little guy is in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know? this one has just become my favourite SCP, I mean, I love classic music, epic music and opera and this one ain't as terrifying as I expected it to be, it's an awesome freak opera singer, and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first thought upon seeing it was Herman Fuller's Circus of the Disquieting? Yay! This is one of my favorite GOIs (maybe because I was around to see its beginning) and I love a good skip done right for the circus. And its musical, so bonus points for Accelerando. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
LCE is too small to be a GOI, since it was only written by me.

Herman-Fuller is technically a GOI, since it has several authors for it. It's also got a tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That picture is quite unsettling, but it's cool how you wrote something slightly more heartwarming out of it.

Why don't Herman Fuller and Light Courier Enterprises have sections on the GoI list?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image: creepy as all fuck.

Skip: sings opera, actually very nice.

Nice job. Also was not anticipating the circus showing up in this one, but it works out well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dammit Soulless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the picture is cute! I kind of want to snuggle with it! The entire thing is really quite adorable, really warmed my heart a lot. A hearty, happy +1 to you!

(Can I take him home with me?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I came in assuming it was going to be a scary monster that ate people or some crap, and instead we have this eerie, strange, paranormal opera singer- Mental imagery and the fact it liked having the Foundation listen to it [to an extent]- None of the cliche Monster What Kills You For X. Just- Yes. You have done good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After a more carefull reading, I realized that there are many other errors in the Italian song. Can I fix'em?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In addition, gallons are Imperial measure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I fixed them all, thanks for pointing them out.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Prego1 c:

Footnotes
1. You are welcome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this concept, and the execution (with regard to article format and flow) is good. However, there are a lot of proofreading errors.

Here are a few...
...that you need to fix.

SCP-1860 is a malnourished humanoid entity of 2.1 meters in height.

Why aren't we feeding it? Can it be malnourished if it doesn't eat? Do you maybe mean 'emaciated'?

to create the material's paper

To create the material's material? To make the paper's paper? Redundancy bugs me.

The source of SCP-1860-1 appears to leak out of microscopic orifices below its waistline.

Either "The source appears to be" or "The material appears to leak". A source cannot leak out of an orifice.

This usually includes only musical notation

So does it "usually include musical notation" or "include only musical notation"? You can't have both.

suspend these forms at 0.6 meters above ground, where it orbits them in a clockwise motion around itself.

Earth does not orbit The Moon around itself. The Moon orbits around Earth.

if called for in the music sung

Sung is the past perfect tense and requires a helper verb. Three ways to fix this. 1. "if called for by the music it is singing." 2. "If called for by the music it has sung." 3. "If called for by the music sang."

There does not to seem any underlying relationships regarding the choice of music

Either "Do not seem to be relationships" or "Does not seem to be any relationship".

Forensics have determined

Same tense confusion. Either "Forensics (a collective singular for either a set of actions or a department) has determined" or "Forensic Agents (a plural) have determined"

Three personnel suffered hearing loss, which brung about the addition of sound-cancelling material into SCP-1860's containment chamber.

"Sing" (present), "Sang" (past), and "Sung" (past perfect) are all correct forms. So are "Ring", "Rang", and "Rung". But in this case it's "Bring", "Brought", and "Brought". "Brung" is not a word.

Normally I'd forgive a few of these and upvote anyway, but there are too many to ignore. Please fix these errors so I can +1 your work in good conscience. I really like the concept but the technical errors are bringing it down.

0 for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For further creepiness, google I Dreamt I Dwelt in Marble Halls, listen to a performance, and picture this bastard singing it. With a smile. :)

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Aaaah sorry i forgot

Thanks for your help! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, so this is what you needed the opera for. I like it. It's a little less creepy then some of the other HFCTD skips, but it's still really unsettling. +1

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Not me for the Italian? :c

P.S.: "La gente amava e SENTIVA."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it! +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I wanted to make a simple, strange thing for Herman-Fuller. Here it is. (Also, this is the first time I've written an SCP about music. Ha.)

Thanks to Roget for reviewing, Tagliafierro for checking the Italian, and PeppersGhost for Herman-Fuller (and reddit user razorsharpgears for the picture!).

Bonus: Look up the second musical piece that's in the table.
